---
title: "LRPGF01b: Gain factors for word reading (W), treated-only"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPGF01b is the **treated-only** companion to LRPGF01 ("gains while on
intervention"): the same gain-factors model for word reading (`ewrswr`) restricted
to on-intervention period rows (it excludes the waitlist arm's untreated period 1).

::: {.callout-important title="No treatment contrast — this is a factor-association model"}

Once restricted to on-intervention rows, the on-intervention indicator is constant,
so the treatment term and its interactions are **dropped**: there is no randomised
contrast here. This model reports the **adjusted associations** of the remaining
factors (own baseline, age, cognitive ability, skills L/R, age $\times$ ability)
with word-reading gains *among the treated*. Under the locked DAG every coefficient
is confounded by latent general ability (the child random intercept repairs the
time-invariant part) — read as "associated with", never "drives". SES is excluded
(non-DAG / redundant). Compare these associations with LRPGF01.

:::

## Model

$$
\operatorname{logit}\mu_{i,p} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}[p]}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(W_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + \gamma_{\text{ability}}\,z(\text{blocks})
  + \sum_{s\in\{L,R\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})
  + \gamma_{\text{int}}\,(\text{age}\times\text{ability})
  + u_{\text{child}[i]},
$$

on the on-intervention rows only, with
$W_{\text{post}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$,
$N = 79$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_cross (ability / skills / interactions)](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=24%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

All terms are **adjusted associations** among the treated (no randomised contrast).
`mean` / `lo` / `hi` are the posterior mean and equal-tailed 95% credible interval
(logit scale); `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef} > 0)$. Interactions are expected to
be weakly identified at $n\approx54$.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
